text: Debugging is an essential skill for any programmer. When you encounter a bug, the first step is to identify where the problem is occurring. A systematic approach can save you a lot of time. Instead of guessing, try to isolate the issue by testing small parts of your code. Many programming environments offer debugging tools like breakpoints and step-through features. These allow you to pause execution and inspect variable values at specific points. Another useful strategy is logging. By adding print statements or logging messages, you can track how data changes over time. This helps you understand the flow of your program and spot unexpected behavior. With practice, debugging becomes much easier.
